original_abstract: Urban traffic congestion has become a pervasive challenge, leading to significant fuel waste, economic productivity loss, and delays in emergency response. Traditional traffic management systems primarily rely on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) using static round-robin scheduling, which fail to adapt to real-time demands. While recent IoT-based solutions have attempted to use traffic density for signal control, their performance is often hindered by a lack of diverse emergency vehicle datasets for training deep neural networks. In this paper, we present a novel distributed IoT framework built upon two key observations. First, recognizing that road infrastructure remains largely static, we develop a two-stage vehicle detector that leverages background subtraction and deep learning to achieve 77% detection accuracy on the UA-DETRAC dataset. Second, we address the identification of emergency vehicles by focusing on their unique acoustic signatures. By implementing a novel siren detection algorithm on edge devices, our system achieves an exceptional emergency vehicle detection accuracy of 99.4%. The integration of these two approaches provides a robust, real-time solution for intelligent traffic management and priority routing.
humanized_abstract: Urban traffic congestion is a ubiquitous problem worldwide, leading to waste of oil, loss of working efficiency, and increased likelihood of accidents caused by delay in emergency service. Generally, existing urban traffic management systems are based on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) with fixed static round-robin scheduling algorithms, which are unable to meet the varying demand of traffic in real time. At present, some intelligent traffic management systems based on IoT have implemented control based on the traffic density in the current section of the road, but the quality of the datasets on emergency vehicles used for training the deep learning model is scarce. In this paper, a new distributed architecture for IoT in intelligent traffic management is proposed based on two aspects. On the one hand, taking the relatively stable condition of the urban roads into account, the vehicle detection algorithm is designed into two stages that first utilize the background subtraction technology, and then the detected objects are further identified using deep learning based on the UA-DETRAC dataset, and the accuracy of the detection result can reach 77\%. On the other hand, according to the characteristics of emergency vehicles that can be identified by the different acoustic signals they produce, an siren detection algorithm is designed for the edge devices, and finally achieves a detection accuracy of 99.4\%. Ultimately, the two stages achieve the goal of efficient intelligent traffic management and optimal emergency route planning.
